channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: The DOJ won't move ahead with anti-weaponization fund, acting AG Todd Blanche says
item.description: The Justice Department is no longer moving ahead with its $1.8 billion anti-weaponization fund. It was a tumultuous couple weeks for a controversial idea that was championed by some MAGA supporters.
item.content:encoded: The Justice Department is no longer moving ahead with its $1.8 billion anti-weaponization fund. It was a tumultuous couple weeks for a controversial idea that was championed by some MAGA supporters.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas

item.title: Heat, bugs, germs: U.S. public health prepares for the World Cup
item.description: For months ahead of the World Cup, states and cities have been preparing to protect travelers and local communities from potential threats including foodborne illness and infectious diseases.
item.content:encoded: For months ahead of the World Cup, states and cities have been preparing to protect travelers and local communities from potential threats including foodborne illness and infectious diseases.
item.dc:creator: Pien Huang

item.title: The mad scramble to find California's next governor becomes clearer today
item.description: It's been a chaotic scramble to find the new leader of California. After former Vice President Kamala Harris decided not to run, there hasn't been a clear candidate to pick up from Gov. Gavin Newsom.
item.content:encoded: It's been a chaotic scramble to find the new leader of California. After former Vice President Kamala Harris decided not to run, there hasn't been a clear candidate to pick up from Gov. Gavin Newsom.
item.dc:creator: Guy Marzorati

item.title: For teenage and young women in Mozambique, DREAMS deferred
item.description: A program focused on HIV prevention specifically for adolescent girls and young women ended following funding cuts by the Trump administration. What do women who benefited from DREAMS have to say?
item.content:encoded: A program focused on HIV prevention specifically for adolescent girls and young women ended following funding cuts by the Trump administration. What do women who benefited from DREAMS have to say?
item.dc:creator: Matt Ozug

item.title: The ancient plant of rivercane makes a comeback for flood control
item.description: To prevent flooding, people in Western North Carolina are turning to a traditional basket weaving ingredient.
item.content:encoded: To prevent flooding, people in Western North Carolina are turning to a traditional basket weaving ingredient.
item.dc:creator: Katie Myers

item.title: Lindsay Davenport on remaining French Open and facing the GOAT
item.description: As the second week of the French Open unfolds, tennis hall of famer Lindsay Davenport shares her takeaways from tennis' second grand slam and Serena Williams' comeback announcement.
item.content:encoded: As the second week of the French Open unfolds, tennis hall of famer Lindsay Davenport shares her takeaways from tennis' second grand slam and Serena Williams' comeback announcement.
item.dc:creator: Jason Fuller

item.title: Trump administration puts out strict Medicaid work rules
item.description: Work rules for sick people on Medicaid will be stricter than advocates had hoped. The Trump administration released its guidance for states, which have to stand up the new bureaucracies by Jan. 1.
item.content:encoded: Work rules for sick people on Medicaid will be stricter than advocates had hoped. The Trump administration released its guidance for states, which have to stand up the new bureaucracies by Jan. 1.
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin

item.title: Author Ann Patchett's new novel makes space for optimism
item.description: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with bestselling author Ann Patchett about her new novel Whistler.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with bestselling author Ann Patchett about her new novel Whistler.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Dorning

item.title: The Trump administration aims to pull science funding under tighter political control
item.description: A Trump administration rule could shift how science funding works in the U.S. The administration says its an effort to deter waste, but science advocates say it will compromise scientific integrity.
item.content:encoded: A Trump administration rule could shift how science funding works in the U.S. The administration says its an effort to deter waste, but science advocates say it will compromise scientific integrity.
item.dc:creator: Katia Riddle

item.title: Rubio testifies on Capitol Hill about Cuba
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio told the Senate Foreign Relations Committee that Cuba needs systemic reforms. But the administration is talking to Cuban officials like ex-leader Raul Castro's grandson.
item.content:encoded: Secretary of State Marco Rubio told the Senate Foreign Relations Committee that Cuba needs systemic reforms. But the administration is talking to Cuban officials like ex-leader Raul Castro's grandson.
item.dc:creator: Michele Kelemen
